signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.03 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 (crowded carry) faces squeeze risk if VIX (15.0) spikes above 25.0. Options market is already pricing protection with a -1.38 25d RR put skew and 3.8pt VRP (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.4%). Carry dominates until energy-driven risk-off triggers a liquidity unwind.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs CB Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5766 — COT LF net short -27,675 (specs fading the kiwi) contradicts the RBNZ's hawkish shift and 'Hike' market bias. With a -0.58% real rate and the first hike in three years delivered, a crowded spec short unwind targets 0.5900+. NZD CESI is neutral (+1), suggesting price action is driven by positioning realignment rather than data surprises.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning + Retail
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4164 — 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage and crowded COT LF net short -87,846 (long%=22.6%) keep the pair bid. USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has collapsed to -0.899 (z=-2.02σ), signaling the pair is ignoring the +4.19% oil spike in favor of USD safe-haven demand. Retail 80% short provides contrarian support for further upside.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Data Divergence + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1397 — 150bp USD carry advantage is reinforced by a widening CESI gap (USD +63 vs EUR -11). EUR COT LF net short -66,057 aligns with persistent data misses. Options market shows elevated hedging demand with a +2.3pt VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.8%), suggesting traders are paying up for protection against further downside ahead of the July 23 ECB meeting.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Fiscal Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3379 — Reports of an expansive UK budget under PM Burnham introduce fiscal uncertainty. While carry is neutral (0bp), the options market maintains a -0.62 25d RR put skew and a significant +5.5pt VRP (IV 11.0% vs HV30 5.5%). Speculative positioning remains net long (+17,228), leaving the pair vulnerable to a long-unwind if the budget triggers gilt volatility.
